title: Fix missing NULL guard for `other` operand in inplace operators (SIGSEGV)
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

The C implementation of all thirteen inplace numeric operators (`+=`, `-=`, `*=`, `%=`, `**=`, `<<=`, `>>=`, `&=`, `^=`, `|=`, `//=`, `/=`, `@=`) on `ObjectProxy` was missing a NULL guard when unwrapping the `other` operand. If `other` was itself a proxy whose `wrapped` attribute had not been set (e.g. created via `__new__` without `__init__`), the code unwrapped it to `NULL` and passed that to the corresponding `PyNumber_InPlace*` function, crashing the interpreter with a SIGSEGV. The non-inplace binary operators already had the correct guard; the same check has now been added to all inplace variants.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/34c9fad09694958b83fbbea19ea30e69
